request.question: What is the type of musical composition that was used by the Korean female band Isak N Jiyeon?

title: Isak N Jiyeon
List of musical forms by era
Pulitzer Prize for Music
Kenzie (songwriter)
Park Kyung-won
Duet
Schillinger System
Lina (South Korean singer)
4 Intimate Nights with Beyoncé
Tell Me Baby (album)
sentences: Isak N Jiyeon (이삭 N 지연) was a Korean female duo formed by SM Entertainment in 2002 that has since been disbanded.
Jiyeon was involved in SM's project named The Grace, a four-member Korean girl group that sings, dances and acts.

Isak was featured in the SM Town albums and videos and continued to do a variety of work, such as being DJ and VJ for Arirang TV, MC work for various networks, and taking on small acting roles on network television.
This is a list of musical forms and genres organized according to the eras of Classical music.
The form of a musical composition refers to the general outline of the composition, based on the sections that comprise it or on specific details that are unique to a certain type of composition.

For example, a rondo is based on alternation between familiar and novel sections (ABACA); a mazurka is defined by its meter and rhythm; a nocturne is based on the mood it creates, required to be inspired by or evocative of night.
This list summarizes these broadly defined forms and genres within the musical periods that they arose or became common.
The Pulitzer Prize for Music is one of the seven American Pulitzer Prizes that are annually awarded for Letters, Drama, and Music.
It was first awarded in 1943.
Joseph Pulitzer did not call for such a prize in his will, but had arranged for a music scholarship to be awarded each year.

This was eventually converted into a full-fledged prize: "For a distinguished musical composition of significant dimension by an American that has had its first performance in the United States during the year."
Because of the requirement that the composition had its world premiere during the year of its award, the winning work had rarely been recorded and sometimes had received only one performance.
In 2004 the terms were modified to read: "For a distinguished musical composition by an American that has had its first performance or recording in the United States during the year."
Kenzie (Korean 켄지 "Kenji", born Kim Yeon Jung, February 3, 1976 in Shimane-ken, Japan) is a composer signed under S.M. Entertainment.
She has composed dozens of songs for S.M. label-mates including BoA, Isak N Jiyeon, Super Junior, The Grace, TVXQ, Girls' Generation, SHINee, f(x), EXO, Red Velvet and NCT.

Park Kyung-won (24 June 1901 – 7 August 1933) was, along with Kwon Ki-ok of the Republic of China Air Force, one of the earliest Korean female aviators. Though it is generally agreed that Kwon was the first female pilot, Park is still recognised as the first Korean female civilian pilot, as Kwon was trained by the Republic of China Air Force.
She was the subject of the controversial 2005 South Korean film "Blue Swallow", in which she was portrayed by actress Jang Jin-young.
A duet is a musical composition for two performers in which the performers have equal importance to the piece, often a composition involving two singers or two pianists.
It differs from a harmony, as the performers take turns performing a solo section rather than performing simultaneously.
A piece performed by two pianists performing together on the same piano is a "piano duet" or "piano four hands".
A piece for two pianists performing together on separate pianos is a "piano duo".
The Schillinger System of Musical Composition, named after Joseph Schillinger (1895–1943) is a method of musical composition based on mathematical processes.
It comprises theories of rhythm, harmony, melody, counterpoint, form, and semantics (emotional meaning, as in movie music).
Lee Ji-yeon (born February 18, 1984), better known by her stage name Lina is a South Korean singer and musical actress.
She debuted in 2002 as a member of South Korean R&B duo Isak N Jiyeon and disbanded in 2004.
She later re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace in 2005 and the group currently being inactive.
She currently active as a musical actress.
4 Intimate Nights with Beyoncé was the second residency show by American recording artist Beyoncé.
Held during four non-consecutive nights in August 2011 at the Roseland Ballroom in New York City, the concerts were part of Beyoncé's campaign in support of her fourth studio album "4" (2011).
All the songs on the standard version of the album, excluding "Start Over", were performed by her to a standing room-only audience of 3,200.
Beyoncé also sang some of her previous hits from her three prior studio albums as well as songs she recorded with former girl group Destiny's Child in the 1990s and early 2000s.
Wearing a linky gold sparkling mini-dress, she was backed by four female dancers and a 20-piece female band including a horn and orchestra section.
Tell Me Baby is the debut and only Korean studio album by South Korean duo Isak N Jiyeon, a new 2002 group from SM Entertainment, released on September 3, 2002.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Produce a single, concise, structured first-hop evidence report as the `summary`. The summary must do all of the following and only include content that helps determine what to fetch next:

- Enumerate concrete facts found in the provided passages. For each fact include the passage source label and either a very short quoted fragment (up to about 15 words) when a decisive short fact appears or a brief paraphrase otherwise. Keep each fact to one short line.

- Explicitly identify the bridge entity(ies) and any missing or ambiguous fact(s) that prevent answering the question from the given passages. If a name or identifier in the question corresponds to multiple candidate pages present in `passages`, list every candidate variant and mark the name as ambiguous; do not choose between them.

- Prescribe concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve each missing or ambiguous element. For each target, specify which page(s) or disambiguation page to fetch and what exact section, keyword, or datum to extract (for example: specific field such as birth year, office held, list membership, dates, or the section heading to check). Prioritize targets (1, 2, 3...) when helpful.

- Only produce a final answer in `summary` if every required fact for every entity is present and unambiguous in the supplied passages; otherwise do not produce a final answer and focus on retrieval guidance.

Formatting and content constraints:
- Keep the summary concise (a short paragraph or a few short bullet-like lines).
- Produce only the single output field `summary` and nothing else.
- Preserve source-linked evidence; do not assert facts without a passage label.
- Do not invent facts or assume which ambiguous candidate is intended.
- Avoid copying long verbatim spans; use short quoted fragments when decisive and paraphrase otherwise.
- When an expected authoritative page is missing from `passages`, note that absence and include that page title as a next-hop retrieval target.

Input: fields `question` and `passages`. Output: one field `summary` meeting the requirements above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

You are to produce exactly one compact second-hop retrieval query that targets the missing bridge or supporting evidence referenced by summary_1. Follow these operational rules when constructing the single `query` string:

- Use visible anchor tokens from `question` or `summary_1` (named works, titles, artist names, years or other identifiers only if present) and append a short retrieval cue naming the bridge sought (e.g., artist, album, recording, genre, season count, passage, supporting quote).
- Emit one concise line (one string assigned to `query`), roughly ≤ 10–12 words, formatted either as a short interrogative that names the anchors or as a compact keyword string (anchors + bridge keywords).
- If summary_1 explicitly requests a missing attribute or passage, emit the focused retrieval query rather than asking for clarification.
- Do not include answers, conclusions, enumerations, provenance notes, speculative language, or user-facing explanations; do not invent entities or add tokens that are not present in the inputs.
- Include year or other identifier tokens only if they appear in the provided inputs and help disambiguate.
- Keep wording retrieval-oriented and terse. Preserve the fallback that if retrieval using this query later fails, downstream steps may request clarification.

Output contract: produce exactly one field `query` whose value is the single compact retrieval query line described above.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single field only):
1. Merge the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into one compact integrated `summary` that preserves the primary answer name(s) and only includes passage-supported facts needed to justify or disambiguate that answer.
2. If `context` already contains a concise answer (a short sentence or a name), keep that content as the first sentence of `summary` when it is consistent with facts in `passages`. If `context` lacks a concise answer, create a concise first sentence that names the primary answer(s), using the canonical labels from `context` when available and otherwise from `passages`.
3. Expand the summary only when `passages` provide explicit, matching second-hop evidence about one or more named entities referenced in `context`. When expansion is appropriate, append at most one short supporting sentence per named entity. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's full name as shown in the inputs and paraphrase up to a few core attributes that are directly present in the `passages` (examples: nationality, a notable award/status, a current affiliation/team, a birth date, membership/partnership relation, or an explicit founding/formation fact).
4. Keep `summary` concise: typically 1–2 sentences for a single entity; allow up to 3 sentences only when multiple distinct named entities each require one supporting sentence. Prioritize the facts most directly necessary to answer the `question`.
5. Only assert facts that are directly supported by the provided `passages` or `context`. Do not introduce new entities, dates, awards, affiliations, roles, or causal claims that are not present in the inputs.
6. Prefer paraphrase over long verbatim copying. Use short quoted phrases only when the exact short phrase (roughly ≤10 words) appears in the `passages` and quoting preserves clarity.
7. If `context` and `passages` conflict, prefer facts that are explicitly present in the `passages`: update the concise answer to match passage-supported content rather than preserving a contradicted `context`.
8. If the retrieved `passages` do not supply explicit supporting evidence for named entities in `context`, keep the `summary` concise and do not expand or speculate.
9. Output exactly one field named `summary` whose value is the integrated post-retrieval summary text. Do not output additional fields, metadata, or commentary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Follow these operational rules when producing `answer`:
- Use only the content of `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2` as your evidential basis. Do not introduce new facts or external information.
- If both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, set `answer` to exactly one lowercase token: yes or no. The `answer` field must contain nothing else (no punctuation, capitalization, quotes, explanation, or additional text).
- Decide which token to emit by aggregating the visible summaries only. Treat `summary_2` as the later integrated evidence; do not let unresolved language in `summary_1` alone prevent emitting the single-token result if both summaries now unambiguously agree.
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, incomplete, hedged such that the relation is unresolved, or if the question is non-binary, do not apply the single-token rule; instead follow the agent's existing final-answer fallback behavior for unresolved or non-binary cases (which may produce a fuller response or an uncertainty signal).

Produce only the `answer` field as specified.

response.hop1_query: What is the type of musical composition that was used by the Korean female band Isak N Jiyeon?
hop1_docs: Isak N Jiyeon | Isak N Jiyeon (이삭 N 지연) was a Korean female duo formed by SM Entertainment in 2002 that has since been disbanded.  Jiyeon was involved in SM's project named The Grace, a four-member Korean girl group that sings, dances and acts.  Isak was featured in the SM Town albums and videos and continued to do a variety of work, such as being DJ and VJ for Arirang TV, MC work for various networks, and taking on small acting roles on network television.

Lina (South Korean singer) | Lee Ji-yeon (born February 18, 1984), better known by her stage name Lina is a South Korean singer and musical actress.  She debuted in 2002 as a member of South Korean R&B duo Isak N Jiyeon and disbanded in 2004.  She later re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace in 2005 and the group currently being inactive.  She currently active as a musical actress.
Tell Me Baby (album) | Tell Me Baby is the debut and only Korean studio album by South Korean duo Isak N Jiyeon, a new 2002 group from SM Entertainment, released on September 3, 2002.

Kenzie (songwriter) | Kenzie (Korean 켄지 "Kenji", born Kim Yeon Jung, February 3, 1976 in Shimane-ken, Japan) is a composer signed under S.M. Entertainment.  She has composed dozens of songs for S.M. label-mates including BoA, Isak N Jiyeon, Super Junior, The Grace, TVXQ, Girls' Generation, SHINee, f(x), EXO, Red Velvet and NCT.

Kim Isak | Kim Isak was born Ida Dene Simmons on May 25, 1985 in Frankfurt, Germany.  She left Germany and moved to Kansas with her mother.  She left Kansas when she was 4 1/2 years old to move to California and lived there until 1999.  She emigrated to Korea later to learn about heritage and explore her music career in Korean pop music.  Isak lived in a mostly Caucasian community with her mother.

TTL (Time to Love) | "TTL (Time to Love)" is a hit single by South Korean girl group T-ara and boy band Supernova.  It includes members Eunjung, Hyomin, Soyeon, and Jiyeon of T-ara and Kwangsu, Jihyuk, and Geonil of Supernova.

Colonial Song | Colonial Song is a musical composition written by Australian composer Percy Grainger.  Although Grainger created versions for different types of musical ensembles, its most commonly used version today is for concert band.
response.summary_1:
- Fact: Isak N Jiyeon is "a Korean female duo formed by SM Entertainment in 2002" — passage [1] ("Isak N Jiyeon ... was a Korean female duo formed by SM Entertainment in 2002").
- Fact: Their debut/only Korean studio album is "Tell Me Baby" (released Sept 3, 2002) — passage [3] ("Tell Me Baby is the debut and only Korean studio album ... released on September 3, 2002").
- Fact: Kenzie is "a composer signed under S.M. Entertainment" who "has composed ... including ... Isak N Jiyeon" — passage [4] (paraphrase).
- Fact: "Colonial Song" is "a musical composition written by Australian composer Percy Grainger" and its most common version today is "for concert band" — passage [7] (quoted short fragments: "Colonial Song is a musical composition" ; "most commonly used version today is for concert band").

Bridge entities / missing or ambiguous facts:
- Missing link: No passage states which specific musical composition Isak N Jiyeon "used" (e.g., a sampled/composed/covered work), nor the "type" of any composition they used.
- Ambiguity: The term in the question ("type of musical composition that was used by the Korean female band Isak N Jiyeon") could mean (a) a named composition they performed/used (title), or (b) the ensemble/type (e.g., "concert band," "orchestral arrangement," "R&B song," etc.). Passages do not resolve which meaning is intended.
- Ambiguous name mapping: "Isak N Jiyeon" appears unambiguous in passages [1], [2], [3], [5] — no multiple candidate pages for that band in supplied passages.

Prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve the missing facts:
1) Fetch the full Isak N Jiyeon page (or the SM Entertainment artist page) — extract the "Discography", "Songs", "Track listing", or "Credits" sections (look for song titles, samples, arrangements, and composer/arranger credits). Purpose: identify any specific composition they "used" or performed and the composition's type.
2) Fetch the "Tell Me Baby (album)" page (full article) — extract the track list, songwriting/composition credits, "samples" or "personnel/arrangements" subsection. Purpose: find any named composition or note of arrangement type (e.g., sample of Percy Grainger).
3) Fetch Kenzie (songwriter) detailed credits page — extract the list of songs she composed for Isak N Jiyeon (song titles and roles: composer/arranger). Purpose: determine whether Kenzie supplied a composition type (genre/R&B/pop) used by the duo.
4) If a candidate composition name appears (e.g., a classical piece like "Colonial Song"): fetch that composition's page (e.g., "Colonial Song" / Percy Grainger) — extract the "Arrangements/versions" or opening lines that state ensemble/type (e.g., "for concert band"). Purpose: confirm the composition's type (concert band, orchestral, etc.).
5) If the question intends "genre/type" (e.g., R&B, pop): fetch authoritative genre descriptors on Isak N Jiyeon (lead/artist infobox or genre section) — extract "Genre" field.

No final answer provided here because the supplied passages lack an explicit, unambiguous statement connecting Isak N Jiyeon to a specific composition or composition type.
response.hop2_query: Isak N Jiyeon Tell Me Baby track listing and composition credits
hop2_docs: Tell Me Baby (album) | Tell Me Baby is the debut and only Korean studio album by South Korean duo Isak N Jiyeon, a new 2002 group from SM Entertainment, released on September 3, 2002.

Isak N Jiyeon | Isak N Jiyeon (이삭 N 지연) was a Korean female duo formed by SM Entertainment in 2002 that has since been disbanded.  Jiyeon was involved in SM's project named The Grace, a four-member Korean girl group that sings, dances and acts.  Isak was featured in the SM Town albums and videos and continued to do a variety of work, such as being DJ and VJ for Arirang TV, MC work for various networks, and taking on small acting roles on network television.

If You Love Me, Baby | "Take Out Some Insurance" also known as "Take Out Some Insurance on Me, Baby (If You Love Me, Baby)" or on many releases simply as "Take Out Some Insurance on Me Baby" is a blues song released in 1959 by Jimmy Reed and credited to Jesse Stone.  The copyright registration for the song lists its title as "Take Out Some Insurance on Me, Baby."

Tell Me What You See | "Tell Me What You See" is a song by the Beatles that first appeared in 1965 on their album "Help! " in the United Kingdom and on "Beatles VI" in the United States.  As with all Beatles compositions by either of the two, the song is credited to Lennon–McCartney.  Regarding the song's authorship, Paul McCartney said, "I seem to remember it as mine.  I would claim it as a 60-40 but it might have been totally me."  Lennon said, in his interviews with "Playboy" (1980) and "Hit Parader" (1972), that "Tell Me What You See" was written completely by McCartney.
Don't Tell Me What to Do | "for Baby Animals' song of the same name, see "Don't Tell Me What to Do (Baby Animals song)"
(Baby Tell Me) Can You Dance | "(Baby Tell Me) Can You Dance" was the first single from Shanice's debut album, "Discovery".

Let Me Go, Rock 'n' Roll | "Let Me Go, Rock 'n' Roll" is a song by the American hard rock band Kiss, released in 1974.  It was released as the only single from their second album "Hotter Than Hell".  Even though the song failed to chart, it is a staple in their live concerts.  The B-side was the album title track, "Hotter Than Hell".  Gene Simmons penned the lyrics during a lunch break at his day job, and the song conveys romantic excitement: "'Cause baby's got the feeling/Baby wants a show/Baby won't you tell me/Baby rock & roll, yeah, yeah!" .  "Let Me Go, Rock 'n' Roll" became a live favorite for Kiss.
hop2_titles: Don't Tell Me What to Do
response.summary_2: Isak N Jiyeon is a Korean female duo formed by SM Entertainment in 2002. Their debut and only Korean studio album is Tell Me Baby (released September 3, 2002); the provided passages do not identify any specific musical composition or composition type that the duo "used."
response.answer: The provided summaries do not identify any specific musical composition or composition type used by Isak N Jiyeon.
